senses.g: fog (cloud that forms at a low altitude and obscures vision)
Kohlu (a city in Kohlu district, Balochistan, Pakistan)
Kuwait (a country in West Asia in the Middle East)
Quetta (a city in Quetta district, Balochistan, Pakistan)

کوئی.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit, from Sanskrit कश्चिद् (káścid). Likely also influenced by Sanskrit कोऽपि (kó’pi). Cognate to Awadhi कोई (koī), Braj कोऊ (koū), Punjabi ਕੋਈ (koī), Gujarati કોઈ (koī), Marathi कुणी (kuṇī), Bengali কেউ (keu), Bhojpuri केऊ (kēū), Assamese কেও (keü).

ex.u: کیا یہاں کوئی ہے؟
ex.r: kyā yahā̃ koī hai?
ex.e: Is anyone here?

کوئی.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit, from Sanskrit कश्चिद् (káścid). Likely also influenced by Sanskrit कोऽपि (kó’pi). Cognate to Awadhi कोई (koī), Braj कोऊ (koū), Punjabi ਕੋਈ (koī), Gujarati કોઈ (koī), Marathi कुणी (kuṇī), Bengali কেউ (keu), Bhojpuri केऊ (kēū), Assamese কেও (keü).

senses.g: no worries; no problem; you're welcome

کوئی مسئلہ نہیں.e: Compare Gujarati કોઈ વાંધો નહીં (koī vā̃dho nahī̃), Marathi काही हरकत नाही (kāhī harkat nāhī).

ex.u: مُجھے پَتَہ نَہِیں تھا کِہ تُمہیں سِندھِی آتِی ہَے۔
ex.r: mujhe pata nahī̃ thā ki tumhẽ sindhī ātī hai.
ex.e: I didn't know that you speak Sindhi.

کہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian کِه (ki), from Middle Persian 𐭠𐭩𐭪 (AYK /⁠kū⁠/).

ex.u:
عِشْق پَر زور نَہِیں۔ ہَے یہ وہ آتِش غالِب کِہ لَگائے نَہ لَگے اَور بُجھائے نَہ بَنے۔
'iśq par zor nahī̃. hai ye vo ātiś ġālib ki lagāe na lage aur bujhāe na bane.
ex.r: There is no compulsion in passionate love. It is that fire, Ghālib, that ignites not when ignited and dies not when quenched.

کہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian کِه (ki), from Middle Persian 𐭠𐭩𐭪 (AYK /⁠kū⁠/).
Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀺𑀁 (kiṃ), 𑀓𑀺 (ki), from Sanskrit किम् (kim, “what, which”), probably influenced by the nominative form कः (kaḥ). Doublet of کیا (kyā).

senses.g: an account, narrative (either in past or present)
plot (to a book, movie or some play etc.)

کہانی.e: Feminine of کَہانا (kahānā), first attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi کَہانی (khany), inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀥𑀸𑀡𑀕 (kadhāṇaga), from Sanskrit कथानक (kathānaka). Cognate with Punjabi کَہاݨی (kahāṇī) / ਕਹਾਣੀ (kahāṇī), Gujarati કહાણી (kahāṇī), Marathi कहाणी (kahāṇī), Odia କାହାଣୀ (kāhāṇi), Bengali কাহিনী (kahini), Assamese কাহিনী (kahini).

کہر.e: From Classical Persian کَهَر (kahar, “light brown; bay”).

کہنا.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀥𑁂𑀤𑀺 (kadhedi), from Sanskrit कथयति (katháyati). Cognate with Assamese কোৱা (küa).

کہنی.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀼𑀳𑀡𑀻 (kuhaṇī), from Sanskrit कफोणि (kaphoṇi), कफणि (kaphaṇi), कुफणि (kuphaṇi).

ex.u: اس کی طرح
ex.r: us-kī tarah
ex.e: like him/her

ex.u: کْیا آپ یَہاں رَہْتے ہَیں؟
ex.r: kyā āp yahā̃ rahte ha͠i?
ex.e: Do you live here?

کیا.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀺𑀁 (kiṃ), 𑀓𑀺 (ki), from Sanskrit किम् (kim, “what, which”). Related to کِہ (ki). Compare Nepali क्या (kyā).

senses.g: what? subjective interrogative pronoun used to refer to inanimate objects or abstract entities, also often used to refer to animals

ex.u: آم کا دام کْیا ہَے؟
ex.r: ām kā dām kyā hai?
ex.e: What is the price of mangoes?

کیا.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀺𑀁 (kiṃ), 𑀓𑀺 (ki), from Sanskrit किम् (kim, “what, which”). Related to کِہ (ki). Compare Nepali क्या (kyā).

senses.g: inflection of کرنا (karnā): — masculine singular perfective participle
inflection of کرنا (karnā): — masculine singular perfect indicative
how are you?, how's it going?

کیاری.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑁂𑀆𑀭 (keāra), from Sanskrit केदार (kēdāra, “field”).

کیچڑ.e: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀓𑀺𑀘𑁆𑀘 (*kicca) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀟- (-ḍa-).

senses.g: A state on the Malabar Coast of India, inhabited by the Malayali people.

کیڑا.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀻𑀟 (kīḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit की॒ट (kīṭá).

کیف.e: Borrowed from Russian Ки́ев (Kíjev), perhaps through English Kiev, or another language.

کیفے.e: Borrowed from English café, from French café. Doublet of قہوہ and کافی (kāfī).

senses.g: Calais (a city in Pas-de-Calais department, Hauts-de-France, France)

ex.u: فرانس میں مقامی حکام کا کہنا ہے کہ شمالی ساحلی شہر کیلے میں دو حریف مہاجر گروپوں کے درمیان ہونے والی جھڑپوں میں دس افراد زخمی ہو گئے ہیں۔ کیلے اب بھی برطانیہ جانے کے خواہشمند تارکینِ وطن کے لیے مرکزِ کشش بنا ہوا ہے۔
ex.r: frāns meṉ muqāmī ḥukām kā kahnā hai ke śumālī sāḥilī śahar kaile meṉ do ḥarīf muhājr grūpoṉ ke darmiyan hone vālī jhaṛpoṉ meṉ das afrād zaxmī ho ga'ye haiṉ. kaile ab bhī bart̤āniya jāne ke xvāhiśmand tārikīn-e vat̤an ke li'ye markaz-e kaśiś banā hūa hai.
ex.e: Local officials in France say ten people have been injured in clashes between rival rival refugee groups in the northern coastal city of Calais. Calais is still the center of attraction for immigrants seeking to go to the UK.

کیلے.e: Borrowed from English Calais, with possible influence from French Calais.

کیمیا.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian کیمیا (kīmiyā), from Arabic كِيمِيَاء (kīmiyāʔ), from Ancient Greek χυμεία (khumeía).

ex.u: کیمیائی ہتھیار
ex.r: kīmiyāī hathiyār
ex.e: chemical weapon

کیمیائی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian کیمیائی (kīmiyā'ī), from Arabic كِيمِيائِيّ (kīmiyāʔiyy).

senses.g: snakeskin, specifically that which is sloughed off

ex.u:
سرکنڈوں میں ہے کینچلی اٹکی
کہیں لہرا کے چھپ گیا ہے ناگ
sarkaṇḍõ mẽ hai kẽculī aṭkī
ex.r: kahī̃ lahrā ke chup gayā hai nāg
ex.e: (please add an English translation of this quotation)

کینسر.e: Borrowed from English cancer, from Latin cancer.

کینیا.e: Borrowed from English Kenya, from a dated pronunciation. Possibly also influenced by Arabic كِينِيَا (kīniyā).

کینیڈا.e: Borrowed from English Canada, from the Laurentian kanata (“village, settlement”).

senses.g: inflection of کا (kā): — oblique/vocative masculine singular
inflection of کا (kā): — direct/oblique/vocative masculine plural

ex.u: سَبھی مُشْکِلوں کے باوَجُود، وہ جِیت گَیا۔
ex.r: sabhī muśkilõ ke bāvajūd, vo jīt gayā.
ex.e: In spite of all the difficulties, he won.

کے باوجود.e: Partial calque of Classical Persian بَا وُجُودِ (bā wujūd-i), from باـ (bā-) + وجود (vujūd). By surface analysis, با (bā) + وَجُود (vajūd).

senses.g: near, about, around, next to (in the immediate neighborhood of)

ex.u: دکان میرے گھر کے بغل میں ہی ہے۔
ex.e: The store is somewhere around my house.

ex.u: مَیں چَائے دُودھ کے بِنَا بَنَا رَہَا ہُوں۔
ex.r: ma͠i cāe dūdh ke binā banā rahā hū̃.
ex.e: I am making tea without milk.

ex.u: میں اپنے بھائی کے ساتھ جاؤں گا
ex.e: I will go with my brother.

senses.g: marks a recipient or beneficiary: for
marks purpose: for, to, in order to

کھاتا.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit क्षत्र (kṣatrá, “rule, authority”).

senses.g: inflection of کھانا (khānā, “to eat”): — masculine singular habitual participle
inflection of کھانا (khānā, “to eat”): — masculine singular contrafactual
inflection of کھانا (khānā, “to eat”): — masculine singular oblique habitual participle/masculine plural habitual participle
inflection of کھانا (khānā, “to eat”): — masculine plural contrafactual
a pillar or mound used for marking a boundary

کھاگ.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀔𑀕𑁆𑀕𑁄 (khaggo), from Sanskrit खड्ग (khaḍga).

کھان.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀔𑀸𑀡𑀺 (khāṇi), 𑀔𑀸𑀡𑀻 (khāṇī), from Sanskrit खानि (khāni, “mine”), from the root खन् (khan), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *kan-. Compare Punjabi ਖਾਣ (khāṇ), Gujarati ખાણ (khāṇ), ખાણી (khāṇī), Marathi खाण (khāṇ), and Bengali খাদ (khad). Doublet of کَھانِی (khānī).

کھانا.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀔𑀸𑀤𑀺 (khādi), from Sanskrit खादति (khādati).
Inherited from Prakrit 𑀔𑀸𑀡 (khāṇa), from Sanskrit खादन (khādana, “the act of eating; food”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-). Cognate with Gujarati ખાણું (khāṇũ, “dinner”) and Marathi खाणें (khāṇẽ).

کھانی.e: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit खानि (khāni). Doublet of کَھان (khān).

ex.u: نیمبو کھانے میں کھٹّا ہے۔
ex.e: The lemon is sour to the taste.

کھٹا.e: Borrowed from Punjabi کھٹّا (khaṭṭā, “yellow, sour”), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀔𑀝𑁆𑀝 (khaṭṭa), from Sanskrit खट्ट (khaṭṭa). The expected outcome from direct inheritance is کھاٹا (khāṭā), so the lack of vowel lengthening before a geminate consonant means that the word was probably borrowed from Punjabi.

کھٹائی.e: From Prakrit अइआ, from Sanskrit ता (tā) + इका (-ikā).

کھجلی.e: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀔𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀽𑀮𑀺𑀆 (*khajjūliā), from Sanskrit खर्जु (kharju, “itching”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀮- (-la-) + -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-). Cognate with Assamese খজুলি (khozuli). Doublet of کھاج (khāj) and کَھرْجُو (kharjū).

ex.u: ہر صبح کھجور اور دودھ صحت کے لئے اچھا ہوتا ہے
ex.r: har subah khajūr aur dūdh sehat ke lie acchā hotā hai
ex.e: Dates and milk every morning is good for health

کھجور.e: Inherited from Prakrit खज्जूर (khajjūra), from Sanskrit खर्जूर (kharjūra). Cognate with Gujarati ખજૂર (khajūr), Marathi खजूर (khajūr), Punjabi ਖਜੂਰ / کَھجُور (khajūr), Bengali খেজুর (khejur), Assamese খেজুৰ (khezur).

کھرجو.e: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit खर्जु (kharju). Doublet of کُھجْلِی (khujlī).

senses.g: to go bad; expire (ie. of pickles and foods)
To stand up an object, erect

کھڑکی.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀔𑀟𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀻 (khaḍakkī), from Sanskrit खटक्किका (khaṭakkikā).

senses.g: Khulna (the capital city of Khulna Division, Bangladesh)

کھلنا.e: Urdu verb set کِھلْنا (khilnā) کِھلانا (khilānā) کِھلْوانا (khilvānā) Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀔𑀺𑀮𑁆 (*khil)/*𑀔𑀺𑀮𑁆𑀮𑁆 (*khill) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix). Cognate with Gujarati ખીલવું (khīlvũ).

کھنچنا.e: Urdu verb set کھِن٘چْنا (khĩcnā) کھین٘چْنا (khẽcnā) کھِن٘چْوَانا (khĩcvānā) Intransitive form of کھین٘چْنا (khẽcnā) .

ex.u: مگر اندر قدم رکھو تو سب کھنڈر نظر آئے گا۔ در و بام میں سے اب کچھ سلامت نہیں ہے۔
ex.r: magar andar qadam rakho to sab khanḍar nazar āe gā. dar o bām mẽ se ab kuch salāmat nahī̃ hai.
ex.e: But if you step inside, you will see everything in ruins. Nothing inside is no longer safe or sound.

کھنڈر.e: Inherited from Middle Hindi کهنڐر (khnḍr /⁠khanḍar⁠/) / کهنڐهر (khnḍhr /⁠khanḍhar⁠/), from Sanskrit *खण्डघर (*khaṇḍaghara). Equivalent to کَھنْڈ (khanḍ, “fragment”) + ـر (-ar), from Sanskrit खण्ड (khaṇḍá).

ex.u: اور خود جس کے آگے چار چار نوکر تھے، وہ ایک کھنڈر میں زندگی بسر کر رہی ہیں۔
ex.r: aur xod jis ke āge cār cār naukar the, vo ek khanḍar mẽ zindagī basar kar rahī ha͠i.
ex.e: And the one who was accompanied by several servants, is herself living in a ruin.

senses.g: ruins; or the signs of a ruin (ie. giant ruins of a town)

کھنڈر.e: Inherited from Middle Hindi کهنڐر (khnḍr /⁠khanḍar⁠/) / کهنڐهر (khnḍhr /⁠khanḍhar⁠/), from Sanskrit *खण्डघर (*khaṇḍaghara). Equivalent to کَھنْڈ (khanḍ, “fragment”) + ـر (-ar), from Sanskrit खण्ड (khaṇḍá).

senses.g: a ringing, clinking, jingling, tinkling, clashing or clanking sound

کھنک.e: From کھنکنا (khanaknā, “to clink, jingle, clash, clatter”, verb), which is from Sanskrit खणखणायते (khaṇakhaṇāyate), from खणखणाय (khaṇakhaṇāya, “to utter or give out any peculiar sound, tick, tinkle, crack”, root), which is an onomatopoeia.

کھنکنا.e: Inherited from Sanskrit खणखणायते (khaṇakhaṇāyate), from खणखणाय (khaṇakhaṇāya, “to utter or give out any peculiar sound, tick, tinkle, crack”, root), which is an onomatopoeia.

ex.u: کھوتے کی نِہاری
ex.r: khote kī nihārī
ex.e: donkey nihari (a hoaxical dish stereotypically associated with Lahore)

کھوج.e: Deverbal from کھوجْنا (khojnā, “to seek; search”), inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀔𑁄𑀚𑁆𑀚 (khojja). Compare Gujarati ખોજ (khoj), Maithili खोज (khōj), Bengali খোঁজ (khō̃j), Assamese খোজ (khüz, “step, footprint; ask for”), Odia ଖୋଜ (khoja).

senses.g: inflection of کھوجْنا (khojnā): — second-person singular intimate present imperative

کھوج.e: Deverbal from کھوجْنا (khojnā, “to seek; search”), inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀔𑁄𑀚𑁆𑀚 (khojja). Compare Gujarati ખોજ (khoj), Maithili खोज (khōj), Bengali খোঁজ (khō̃j), Assamese খোজ (khüz, “step, footprint; ask for”), Odia ଖୋଜ (khoja).

کھودنا.e: Derived from Prakrit *𑀔𑁆ō𑀤𑁆𑀤𑁆 (*khōdd). Cognate with Punjabi ਖੋਦਣਾ (khodṇā), Gujarati ખોદવું (khodvũ), Marathi 𑘏𑘻𑘟𑘜𑘹𑘽 (khodṇẽ), Nepali खोदनु (khodanu), Bengali খোদা (khōda).

کھیت.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀔𑁂𑀢𑁆𑀢 (khĕtta), from Sanskrit क्षेत्र॑ (kṣétra), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ćšáytram, from Proto-Indo-European *tḱey- + *-trom.

کھیل.e: From the stem of کھیلنا (khelnā, “to play”). Compare Sanskrit क्रीड (krīḍa).

کھیلا.e: Borrowed from Sanskrit खेला (khelā), ultimately from the root खेल् (khel).

senses.g: inflection of کھیلْنَا (khelnā): — masculine singular perfective participle
inflection of کھیلْنَا (khelnā): — masculine singular perfect indicative: played
to shoot, take a picture (to capture)

ex.u: تَصْوِیر کھین٘چو!
ex.r: tasvīr khẽco!
ex.e: take a photo!

senses.g: to drive in a rush; with speed

کھینچنا.e: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀔𑀺𑀁𑀘𑀇 (*khiṃcaï), 𑀔𑀁𑀘𑀇 (khaṃcaï, “to pull”), from a family of Prakrit words with "dragging" connotations of unknown further origin. Compare Punjabi ਖਿੱਚਣਾ (khiccaṇā) / کِھچّݨا (khiccṇā), Gujarati ખેંચવું (kheñcvũ), Marathi खेचणे (kheċṇe), Bengali খেঁচা (khẽca).

senses.g: The twenty-ninth letter of the Urdu alphabet, written in the Arabic script; preceded by ک and followed by ل.

گاتر.e: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit गात्र (gā́tra), ultimately from the root गा (gā). Doublet of گَات (gāt).

senses.g: practice makes perfect (singing for a very long time inspires the perfect sound)

گاتے گاتے کلاونت ہو جاتے ہیں.r: gāte gāte kalāvant ho jāte ha͠i
گاتے گاتے کلاونت ہو جاتے ہیں.h: गाते गाते कलावंत हो जाते हैं

گاجر.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀭 (gajjara), from Sanskrit गार्जर (gārjara). Doublet of گزر (gazar).

گاڑی.e: Inherited from Prakrit गड्ड (gaḍḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-). Cognate with Punjabi گَڈِّی (gaḍḍī).

گاڑھا.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀸𑀠 (gāḍha), from Sanskrit गाढ (gāḍha), from the root गाह् (gāh). Cognate with Nepali गाढा (gāḍhā), Sindhi ڳاڙهو (g̠āṛho) / ॻाढ़ो, Gujarati ગાઢું (gāḍhũ), Marathi गाढ (gāḍh), Kholosi gahro.

گال.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀮𑁆𑀮 (galla), from Sanskrit गल्ल (galla), possibly from Proto-Dravidian [Term?].

گانا.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀸𑀅𑀤𑀺 (gāadi), from Sanskrit गाय॑ति (gā́yati).

گانتھ کا پورا عقل کا اندھا.c: گانٹھ کا پورا عقل کا اندھا
گانتھ کا پورا عقل کا اندھا.h: गाँठ का पूरा अक़्ल का अंधा

گانجا.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀕𑀁𑀚 (gaṃja), from Sanskrit गञ्जा (gañjā).

senses.g: a surname, Gandhi, originating as an occupation
a surname, Gandhi, originating as an occupation — Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi (1869–1948)

گاندھی.e: Inherited from Middle Hindi گاندھی (gandhy /⁠gāndhī⁠/) (c. 1654), from Apabhramsa गंधीय (gaṃdhīya), from Prakrit 𑀕𑀁𑀥𑀺𑀅 (gaṃdhia), from Sanskrit गान्धिक (gāndhika). Proper noun sense 1.1 is a semantic loan from Gujarati ગાંધી (gāndhī).

Inherited from Middle Hindi گاندھی (gandhy /⁠gāndhī⁠/) (c. 1654), from Apabhramsa गंधीय (gaṃdhīya), from Prakrit 𑀕𑀁𑀥𑀺𑀅 (gaṃdhia), from Sanskrit गान्धिक (gāndhika). Proper noun sense 1.1 is a semantic loan from Gujarati ગાંધી (gāndhī).

گانڈ.e: Inherited from Sanskrit गण्ड (gaṇḍá). Cognate with Assamese গাঁৰ (gãr).

گانڈ پھٹنا.e: Literally, “to have one's anus tear up”.

senses.g: shithead, fucker, son of a bitch (a general insult)
obsolete form of گاؤں (gāõ) - oblique plural of گان٘و (gā̃o)

گاو.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَاو (gāw). Doublet of گائے (gāe).

ex.u: پنجاب کے ضلع گورداسپور میں ایک گاؤں نارتھ ویسٹرن ریلوے کے سٹیشن بٹالہ سے بفاصلہ ۱۰۔ میل واقع ہے۔
ex.r: ʻAbdurrashīd
ex.e: A village in the Gurdaspur district of Punjab which lies 10 miles away from the North Western railway station of Batala.

گاؤں.e: Inherited from Old Hindi گانوں (gā̃õ) / گاؤں (gāõ) / گانو (gā̃v), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀕𑀸𑀫 (gāma), from Sanskrit ग्राम (grā́ma), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *grā́mas, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *grā́mas, from Proto-Indo-European *(h₂)gróm-os, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂ger- (“to assemble, gather together”).

senses.g: at times, sometimes, from time to time, not often, occasionally

گاہے بہ گاہے.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian گاہ به گاہ (gāh ba-gāh, “sometimes”).

گائے.e: From Sanskrit गो (go), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *gāuš, from Proto-Indo-European *gʷṓws.

گپ.e: Either onomatopoeic and inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀕𑀧𑁆𑀧- (*gappa-), or inherited from Sanskrit गल्प (galpa). Cognate with Classical Persian گَپ (gap), Punjabi ਗੱਪ (gappa) / گپّ (gappa), Bengali গপ (gop), Gujarati ગપ (gap), Nepali गफ (gaph), Odia ଗପ (gapa), Assamese গপ (gop).

گٹھری.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀕𑀁𑀞𑀺 (gaṃṭhi) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀭- (-ra-) + -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-), from Sanskrit ग्रन्थि (granthi).

senses.g: Gajjar Mashkay (a city in Awaran district, Balochistan, Pakistan)

گجرا.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀕𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀭 (gajjara), from Sanskrit गार्जर (gārjara, “carrot”). Doublet of گاجَر (gājar). Compare Marathi गजरा (gajrā).

گجرات.e: Borrowed from Punjabi گُجْرات (gujrāt), derived from the name of the Gujjars.
Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀼𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀭𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀸 (gujjarattā), from Sanskrit गुर्जरत्रा (gurjaratrā, “country of the Gurjaras”), from गुर्जर (gurjara, “Gurjaras”).

senses.g: Gujarati (of, from or relating to the state of Gujarat, India)
Gujarati (native or inhabitant of the state of Gujarat, India)

گدھ.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀺𑀤𑁆𑀥 (giddha), from Sanskrit गृध्र (gṛ́dhra). Compare Gujarati ગીધ (gīdh), ગીધડું (gīdhḍũ), Marathi गिधाड (gidhāḍ).

ex.u: حضور نے ایک بار دیکھا کہ کسی شخص نے ایک گدھے کا منہ داغا تھا بے چارے کی ناک سے مسلسل خون بہہ رہا تھا۔
ex.r: rauśnī
ex.e: His highness once noticed a man who had stained his donkey's face, the poor [thing]'s nose was constantly bleeding.

گدھا.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀕𑀤𑁆𑀤𑀳 (gaddaha), from Sanskrit गर्दभ (gardabha). Compare Saraiki گَڈّاں (gaḍḍāṉ) / ڳَݙّا (g̈aḏḏā) and Sindhi گَڏَھُ (gaḏahu).
Inherited from Prakrit 𑀕𑀤𑁆𑀤𑀳 (gaddaha), from Sanskrit गर्दभ (gardabha). Compare Saraiki گَڈّاں (gaḍḍāṉ) / ڳَݙّا (g̈aḏḏā) and Sindhi گَڏَھُ (gaḏahu).

senses.g: a fool; she-ass – feminine of گَدھا (gadhā)

ex.u:
Kabira [encore], Yeh Jawāni Hai Dīwānī
....گُڑیا رے گُڑیا تیرا گُڈّا پَردیسِیا جوڑی آسمانی ہوگی شَگُن پِہ دیکھو شادمانی ہوگی ...
....guṛyā re guṛyā terā guḍḍā pardesiyā joṛī āsmānī hogī śagun pe dekho śādmānī hogī ...
ex.e: Oh doll oh doll, your male-doll is a foreigner. Your match/pairing has become heavenly. On the omen, look happiness has become.

گذارنا.e: From Classical Persian گذار (guzār) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

senses.g: to pass before, to put or lay before, to present
to pass forward, to adduce, to produce

گذراننا.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian گذران (guzarān) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

senses.g: to pass (by, over or through), to overlook
to come to pass, to befall

گذرنا.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian گذر (guzar) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

ex.u: شیر کی گرج سنو
ex.r: šēr kī garaj suno!
ex.e: listen to the roar of the lion!

گرجا.e: Borrowed from Portuguese igreja, from Old Galician-Portuguese igreja, a semi-learned borrowing from Latin ecclēsia, borrowed from Ancient Greek ἐκκλησία (ekklēsía), from ἔκκλητος (ékklētos) + -ῐ́ᾱ (-ĭ́ā). Doublet of کَلِیسِیا (kalīsiyā).

گرجنا.e: Adapted from Sanskrit गर्जति (gárjati). Doublet of گَاجْنَا (gājnā). Compare Maithili गरजब (garᵊjab) / 𑒑𑒩𑒖𑒥 (garajaba), Punjabi ਗਰਜਣਾ (garjaṇā) / گَرجݨا (garjṇā), Gujarati ગરજવું (garajvũ), Marathi गरजणे (garajṇe).

گردن.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَرْدَن (gardan). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi گردن (grdn /⁠gardan⁠/).

senses.g: a weapon wielded by Faridun in the Shahnameh

گرز.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُرْز (gurz). In the Indo-Persian tradition, it became associated with lightning-bolts, thunder and storms. It became a metaphor for strength and force as well, especially in relation to the warrior and fighter.

ex.u: ارے ارے چلو، تم تو گرگٹ سے تیز رنگ بدلتے ہو
ex.r: Are are calo tum to girgiṭ se tez rang badalte ho
ex.e: Oh, come on! You change colors faster than a chameleon!

گرم خانہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَرْمْخَانَه (garmxāna, “greenhouse”), by analysis گَرْم (garm, “hot”) + خانَہ (xāna, “place”).

senses.g: a towel, a hand tool for lifting and dressing mortar plaster etc., mason's trowel

گرمکھی.e: Borrowed from Punjabi ਗੁਰਮੁਖੀ (gurmukhī, “of or relating to the Guru-oriented people”).

گرمی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَرْمِی (garmī), from Middle Persian 𐭢𐭥𐭬𐭩𐭧 (gʿmyḥ /⁠garmīh⁠/), from Proto-Iranian *garmáh, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *gʰarmás, from Proto-Indo-European *gʷʰer- + *-mós. First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi گرمی (grmy /⁠garmī⁠/). By surface analysis, گَرْم (garm) + ی (ī).

گروہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُروه (gurōh), from Middle Persian 𐭢𐭥𐭥𐭤 (glwh’ /⁠grōh⁠/, “group, crowd”). Compare Sindhi گِروهَه (girohah).

گڑ.e: Inherited from Old Hindi गुड (guḍa), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀼𑀟 (guḍa), from Sanskrit गुड (guḍá). Cognate to Punjabi ਗੁੜ (guṛ), Gujarati ગોળ (goḷ), Marathi गूळ (gūḷ), Bengali গুড় (guṛ).

ex.u:
Kabira [encore], Yeh Jawāni Hai Dīwānī
... گُڑیا رے گُڑیا تیرا گُڈّا پَردیسِیا جوڑی آسمنی ہوگئی شَگُن پے دیکھو شادمانی ہوگئی ...
... guṛyā re guṛyā terā guḍḍā pardesiyā joṛī āsmnī hogī śagun pe dekho śādmānī hogī ...
ex.e: Oh doll oh doll, your male-doll is a foreigner. Your match/pairing has become heavenly. On the omen, look happiness has become.

گڑھ.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀕𑀠 (gaḍha), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *gʰr̥dʰás, from Proto-Indo-European *gʰerdʰ-.

ex.u: میری آپ سے ایک گزارش ہے۔
ex.r: merī āp se aik guzāriś hai.
ex.e: I have a request for you.

گزر.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian گزر (gazar). Doublet of گاجر (gājar).

گستاخی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُسْتَاخِی (gustāxī). By surface analysis, گُسْتَاخ (gustāx) + ـی (-ī).

senses.g: practice by the Tablighi Jamaat of visiting local Muslims in the vicinity and invite them to the mosque
a circular (a letter to be circulated)
